what_it_does: Renders the operator's main inbox surface as a tone-coded triage deck. It partitions live escalations into build-intent, deferred-offer, and action-grouped lanes, reads a held-build deferred queue from the event log, and shows a collapsible resolved-for-you audit, wiring each card to review actions such as openCoa, startWalk, reviveOffer, and resolveUiTarget.
observations: Uses shared kit primitives (SectionHead, LaneHead, Badge, Surface, EmptyState) for a card-based layout.
Splits live_escalations into builds via isBuildIntent, deferred offers via action === 'deferred_offer', and remaining items grouped by action with a test-pollution heuristic.
Builds are rendered with BuildIntentCard inside PanelErrorBoundary; other rows use Surface cards.
The deferred queue is derived from events of kind decision.deferred, deduped by derived_from, and filtered out once the corresponding seq dispatches.
Resolved items live in a collapsible green lane toggled by showResolved.
Preserves data-ui-ref and ui:// addresses for inbox, deferred-queue, inbox/walk, inbox/build-review, inbox/coa, and inbox/target.
A comment notes the deferred-offers lane intentionally omits a data-ui-ref because ui://inbox/deferred-offers is not yet registered.
startWalk is offered only when there are live escalations and no active session, and is disabled while wtBusy.
standouts: Recognition-by-sight design: lanes are distinguished by tone (wire, await, dim, sig) rather than relying on text alone.
Two distinct deferred concepts are kept separate: resumable deferred_offer cards vs held-build decision.deferred queue.
Test-pollution items are visibly separated into their own dimmed lane via a regex heuristic.
Click-to-thing navigation button inside action rows uses stopPropagation to avoid triggering the row's openCoa.
conventions: React functional component consuming AppContext via useApp.
Derived collections computed in render body from context state.
data-ui-ref / ui:// addressing for testability and navigation targets.
Error boundaries around rich interactive cards.
Inline comments annotate preserved behavior and design rationale.
concerns: Heavy use of any weakens type safety.
The test-pollution regex is heuristic and may miscategorize production items.
The deferred-offers lane lacks a registered data-ui-ref, which the code acknowledges.
Surface cards receive dataUiRef as a prop rather than a data-ui-ref DOM attribute; whether this renders correctly depends on the kit implementation.
No loading or error state for context data; empty state only covers zero escalations with no builds.
notes: This is the redesigned inbox reference surface: tone-coded lanes built from the shared kit, with explicit separation between live escalations, parked offers, held builds, and resolved audits, while preserving existing interaction addresses and review flows.
questions: Does the Surface kit component forward dataUiRef to a data-ui-ref DOM attribute?
Should reviveOffer receive the full escalation object rather than just d.id?
Are events and types stable enough to avoid re-deriving lanes on every render?
Is the test regex intended to be inclusive enough for all test/fixture naming conventions?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's stated purpose is to redesign the inbox into a recognition-by-sight triage deck, and the code does exactly that while preserving the listed behavioral contracts.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud visibility of held and deferred work
Separation of test pollution from operational items
intention_signals: Move the inbox from a bare list to a scannable triage deck where color and shape convey workload, keep all existing interaction addresses and review flows intact, and make parked offers, held builds, and resolved audits honest and accessible.
novelty: The explicit distinction between two deferred concepts—resumable interactive deferred_offer cards and concurrency-held decision.deferred builds—rendered as separate lanes with different affordances.
contribution: A tone-coded, card-based inbox layout that groups escalations by lifecycle state and action, surfaces held builds and resolved audits without hiding them, and preserves data-ui-ref/ui:// navigation hooks for operator review workflows.
summary_for_embedding: Inbox.tsx is a React component that renders the operator's main triage surface as a tone-coded deck of shared kit cards, separating live escalations into build-intent, deferred-offer, action, held-build, and resolved-for-you lanes with preserved ui:// navigation and review actions.
intention_for_embedding: Redesign the operator inbox from a bare list into a recognition-by-sight triage deck while keeping existing interaction addresses, review flows, and fail-loud visibility of held and resolved items.

symbols.does: Renders the operator inbox triage surface with lifecycle lanes and review actions.
symbols.description: The Inbox component consumes AppContext state, partitions live escalations into build intents, deferred offers, and action-grouped lanes, renders a held-build deferred queue from the event log, and provides a collapsible resolved-for-you audit. It wires row clicks to openCoa, startWalk, reviveOffer, and resolveUiTarget, and wraps build cards in PanelErrorBoundary.

symbols.does: Heuristically flags escalations as test, fixture, pollution, sample, or demo items.
symbols.description: A local helper that concatenates the escalation's payload name and id and tests against a case-insensitive regex for test, fixture, pollut, sample, or demo. Items matching this prefix are placed in a separate dimmed action lane so test pollution remains visible but distinct from operational escalations.
